w. ]. Spillman. agrostologist of the
department of agriculture. gives a
careful account of very useful
method OF curing hay. Harvesting
on this farm Is a very interesting
process" says Mr.. spilIman. THE
method Of curing is as follows:


1.-THE grass Is cut in the afternoon.
The first nights dew never hurts 1t.
Let 1t die the next day until noon.
II Is then put into curing cocks.
which are made to lie nat. These
cokcs are upset the next morning.
and In the afternoon four of them
are made into one weathering cock.
Let 1t stand thus for one day: then
haul to the barn or rick


AS there are many persons these
days who are turning to farming
with little or no experience, the ex-
plicit directions given by Mr. Spill
man will doubtless prove most valu-
able. although, of course. they may
require some modification with vary
ing conditions Of weather and quality
OF hay.
